name: Greek Breakfast Pitas
method: Heat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Organize bacon on the paper in a thin layer.
Place in the preheated oven and cook for 10 to 15 minutes before the
optimal amount of crispiness is achieved.
Move bacon to a lined plate of clean cloth to soak and retain bacon
Place it in a bowl of Greek yogurt.
Halve 1 cucumber and grind part of it into the yogurt.
To create tzatziki dip, whisk in garlic powder, white pepper, and salt.
The leftover cucumber can be sliced into thin pieces and put aside.
Over moderate flame, heat a skillet.
Insert the sausage; roast and stir for five minutes, until golden
brown.
Switch to a pan.
Move the bacon fat over medium-high heat to a pan.
Insert pita rounds; roast on each side once lightly browned,
about 30 seconds. Absorb on towels.
Cover with sausage and bacon from each pita.
On each pita, arrange tzatziki dip, spring mixture, Roma
tomato, cucumber pieces, and onions.
In a shallow pan, add the remaining tablespoon of oil.
Crack in one egg; cook approximately 1½ minutes per side
once set but still tender.
Place the egg on a baked pita top.
Continue with the oil and eggs available.
